name: 10q26_3_subtelomeric_recurrent_CNV
description: 10q26.3 distal subtelomeric deletion / duplication region. Recurrent terminal deletions (1-12 Mb) cause 10q26 deletion syndrome (ID, growth retardation, congenital anomalies, craniofacial features). Subtelomeric SD blocks at 10q26.3 mediate a subset of breakpoints. DMBT1 and FGFR2 lie within frequently deleted regions; TLX1 lies more proximally on 10q24.31.

architecture.internal_structure: Subtelomeric SD cluster (~50-200 kb blocks) shared with other subtelomeric regions; critical dosage-sensitive gene candidates include DOCK1, FGFR2 (more proximal), TCERG1L; DMBT1 paralog cassette contributes to variable breakpoints.
